message:
test(e2e): add OpenAI connector E2E compliance, invoke, and streaming test suite

Add a feature-gated E2E test module for the OpenAI connector behind
the `openai` feature flag in the fcp-e2e crate.

Test infrastructure:
- OpenAiConnectorAdapter bridging the connector's method-based API to
  the FcpConnector trait for the E2E test runner
- Helper functions for manifest generation, handshake requests, token
  building, invoke requests, host validation, and SSE body construction

Four E2E test scenarios:
- openai_default_deny_compliance_suite_passes: verifies correct denial
  when capability tokens don't match (default-deny posture)
- openai_allow_valid_token_connector_suite_passes: full happy-path with
  wiremock (configure -> handshake -> invoke with valid capability token)
- openai_manifest_network_guard_allows_openai_and_denies_non_openai_hosts:
  validates manifest network constraints only allow api.openai.com
- openai_streaming_backpressure_is_deterministic: SSE streaming test
  with 8ms artificial backpressure verifying deterministic 4-chunk
  assembly of "Hello world"

New dev-dependencies: fcp-crypto, fcp-openai, futures-util, wiremock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
